subject: English
chapter: A Homage to Our Brave Soldiers

activities_projects.title: Qualities of Brave Soldiers
activities_projects.objective: Identify qualities that make soldiers courageous and selfless
steps: Read the chapter carefully
Underline or note down the qualities mentioned
List additional qualities you think are essential for a soldier
Discuss your list with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the personal traits of bravery, patriotism, and sacrifice
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of qualities and active participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Patriotism Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Discuss the meaning of patriotism and dedication
steps: Form small groups
Discuss examples from the chapter showing soldiers’ patriotism
Share personal thoughts on serving the country
Present group ideas to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students appreciate the importance of serving and respecting the nation
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of ideas shared in discussion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Riddle / Brain Teaser Activity
activities_projects.objective: Enhance reasoning while connecting to the theme of courage
steps: Solve riddles related to bravery and soldiers
Work in pairs to discuss and find answers
Write answers with reasoning in the notebook
Share answers with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop logical thinking related to the chapter theme

activities_projects.title: Character Sketch of Soldiers
activities_projects.objective: Understand the characteristics of soldiers through examples
steps: Identify soldiers mentioned in the chapter
List their traits, such as bravery, teamwork, and sacrifice
Provide examples from the text showing these traits
Discuss your character sketches with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how courage and patriotism are reflected in actions
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and examples in the character sketch (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play / Skit Activity
activities_projects.objective: Express understanding of soldiers’ bravery through performance
steps: Form groups and assign roles (soldiers, family members, leaders)
Recreate a scene showing soldiers’ courage and dedication
Perform the scene for the class
Discuss the values demonstrated
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve communication, teamwork, and empathy

activities_projects.title: Poem / Prose Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Recognise literary expressions that honour soldiers
steps: Read the poem or prose sections paying attention to expressive words
Identify phrases that depict bravery and sacrifice
Discuss the impact of these words in conveying patriotism
Write your analysis in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance comprehension and vocabulary related to emotion and heroism
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and analysis of literary expressions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sound Words / Onomatopoeia
activities_projects.objective: Learn words that imitate sounds related to army or battlefield
steps: Identify sound words in the chapter (e.g., boom, clang, whistle)
Use them in sentences describing bravery or action scenes
Share your sentences with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the use of sound words in narration
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct usage of sound words (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Writing a Letter to a Soldier
steps: Imagine writing a letter to a soldier thanking them for their service
Include questions about their experience and words of encouragement
Write the letter with proper format and tone
Optionally, share letters in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative writing and emotional expression
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, content, and format of the letter (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Conditional Sentences Practice
activities_projects.objective: Understand if-clauses in the context of soldiers’ scenarios
steps: Study examples of conditional sentences in the chapter
Create sentences like 'If a soldier is brave, he protects his country'
Write your own conditional sentences and share with peers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve grammar skills and contextual understanding

activities_projects.title: Narrative Essay Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop writing skills by telling stories of bravery
steps: Choose a topic such as 'A Brave Soldier I Admire' or 'The Day I Felt Proud of Our Army'
Write a narrative essay with introduction, body, and conclusion
Include descriptive details and emotions
Share your essay with the teacher or class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve narrative writing, creativity, and patriotic reflection
activities_projects.evaluation: Structure, creativity, and grammar in essay (10 marks)
